scope: QA review-only audit (no source modifications) on collaboration-bus and worker/commander/monitor/merger behaviors.

findings: Worker results use agent_id but monitor/merger expect worker_id, breaking attribution and runtime stats linkage.
runtime-stats.json is never created; monitor only updates if it already exists, so scoring/telemetry stays empty.
Status vocabulary inconsistent: worker done vs commander success; merge script accepts only done while monitor treats success/done as success.
Commander bundle lacks explicit fields for adopted collab consensus item_ids/source_refs though launcher requires them.
Collab bus JSONL has no validation; malformed lines can poison parsing; concurrent append risk noted.
Default-branch assumption main in files_changed and diff scoring can yield empty change lists on non-main repos.
stampede.sh uses .tmp suffix while docs/monitor/merge expect .tmp- prefix; inconsistency undermines temp-file filtering assumptions.
stampede.sh uses os.replace vs docs/agents using os.rename; behavior diverges (minor but undocumented).
Commander agent lacks explicit atomic write code snippet/checklist, increasing risk of non-atomic bundle writes.
Requeue of claimed tasks only triggers when live_agents == 0; dead-agent claims persist if any agent still alive.
Teardown does not reconcile claimed tasks; claims can remain stranded.
Monitor can idle with queued tasks and no agents after recovery.
Consensus is captured in collab JSONL ledgers but not consumed by orchestrator or merger.
No worker-level consensus signals or cross-agent voting/semantic conflict detection.
Commander bundles include consensus tier but downstream ignores it.
No blocked/partial status defined in schemas; monitor treats non-done/non-success as failure.
Commander partial status is not surfaced; monitor only warns when status==success but incomplete proof.

top_risks: Result schema mismatches (worker_id vs agent_id; status done vs success) break attribution and merge inclusion.
Atomic write naming inconsistencies and missing commander atomic guidance risk partial/corrupt bundles.
Consensus data captured but unused, losing critical multi-agent agreement signals.
Claimed-task cleanup can leave stranded tasks in claimed/ with stale metadata.
quick_wins: Standardize result schema fields (worker_id/agent_id) and status vocabulary across worker/commander/merge.
Create runtime-stats.json skeleton early in runs.
Align atomic write naming to .tmp- prefix everywhere; add commander atomic write snippet.
Incorporate collab consensus into orchestrator synthesis and merger scoring.
